top_emotional_moments.text: If you'd like to be able to listen to the show without ads,
Sean, please give us a brief bio on yourself.
So I've had my fair share of trauma events over the last decade and a half or so.
and fell in love with Texas and decided to stay.
Ended up going through some little college, didn't really know what I wanted to do,
So, Sean, you created Lone Star Cryptid Research.
Well, just started it pretty much in the spring to try to document things I find and stuff out in the field.
I had really not heard much about Dogman.

segments.text: Thank you.
Thank you.
If you'd like to be able to listen to the show without ads,
and have full access to bonus content, that's an option.
To find out how, please go to dogmanencounters.com forward slash podcast.
Tonight's guest is Sean Brackenridge.
Sean, welcome to the show.
Thank you for having me, Vic.
Well, thanks for being here.
We appreciate your time.
Sean, please give us a brief bio on yourself.
I'm 48, ex-military.
I work for the government right now, mainly with the military reserves, helping them be medically ready for deployments and things like that.
I just came off.
I've been doing this for about two years.
Worked as a in-fire and EMS for 13 years, mainly EMS as an EMT.
So I've had my fair share of trauma events over the last decade and a half or so.
and spent about eight years in the Army Infantry.
Came down, grew up in West Virginia and moved to Texas after I got stationed here from Fort Benning, Georgia,
and fell in love with Texas and decided to stay.
Ended up going through some little college, didn't really know what I wanted to do,
and ended up getting into fire and EMS thanks to a buddy who went in and enjoyed it.
So, and then I got into it and enjoyed it for as long as I could until, you know, physically, emotionally tore me down.
So, decided to get out and try something different and take more of a desk job.
Now, I understand.
Yeah, you're in for a long time.
And, of course, thank you so much for your service.
I really appreciate it.
Not a problem.
I definitely do.
So, Sean, you created Lone Star Cryptid Research.
What can you tell us about LCR?
Well, just started it pretty much in the spring to try to document things I find and stuff out in the field.
I got hooked up with a buddy.
His name is Nick Lasoya.
It was kind of accidental in a sense.
We were on a Bigfoot page on Facebook and had posted something, and he started talking to me, and he direct contacted me.
And we just started talking, and he had asked me if I had seen the Small Town Monsters documentary of the Dogman Triangle of Texas.
And I was like, yeah, I think I just watched that just recently.
Pretty interesting stuff, because I had just started getting into Dogman earlier, well, pretty much late, mid-last year.
I had really not heard much about Dogman.
I grew up as a Bigfoot fan and having a fascination with Bigfoot thanks to the PG film.

But just over the last couple of years, especially listening to Bigfoot Society and Jeremiah talking about people seeing these creatures out in the woods that have the head of a dog and the body of a man.
So that captivated me.

And then when I met Nick he was in Aaron Deese documentary The Dog Man Triangle talking about some of the Native American lore that his dad passed down from him to him from their tribe and stuff like that And we just got to talking and he was like I need a research partner He like
would you be willing to go out with me?
And I was like,
So we delved as deep into it as possible.
Cause out in the area where he lives North,
well about Northwest,
about an hour Northwest of San Antonio,
which is Lake Medina,
there is a hotbed of activity out there, especially for dogmen.
And there has been a few Bigfoot sightings.
They're a little bit rarer compared to the dogman stuff.
But we've been going and spending a lot of time out in the areas out there trying to
find clues and evidence and things like that and try to figure out what these things really
are or at least educate ourselves.
but just both of us have a huge fascination with them.
we just get out there pretty much every weekend and do as much research as we
can and explore as much as we can.
It's funny.
Most guys,
I'm not going to say most guys,
but a lot of guys on the weekend,
they want to head out and go fishing or hunting or whatever.
Watch some football,
you name it.
But you and Nick,
you guys head out and look for dog men.
That's a different kind of pastime.
I grew up around guns and stuff.
My dad owns a gun business back home in West Virginia.
So I grew up around guns and stuff.
So I like to get out and shoot and I like to be out in nature.
I love hiking.
I used to pretty much go hiking every weekend I was off.
And, you know, kind of like unknowingly going out in some of these hiking areas and not even knowing what's exactly out there.
And now that I'm more cued into things that people see and things that I've seen already, it's changed my aspect of thinking about what's out there.
And it has grown into an obsession now because I just want to know the history of these things, what they are, why they are, you know, as much as I possibly can.
And I know that's probably an answer I'll never really get the full answer to, but they're intriguing creatures.
And, you know, the ones that we have come across so far in that area out there have it seem to be aggressive.
But that doesn't mean they won't be if they're provoked.
So we try not to go out and provoke them, but we try to, you know, make our presence known.
But we let them kind of come to us and get to know us in a way where we're not aggressive towards them or anything like that.
Because Nick always says that the more we're out there, the more they get to know our scent, know what we're like,
and whether or not we're a threat to them or not.
So the less we're a threat to them, he thinks the more, you know,
they'll make their presence known to us and let us see them more often.
And since January, since I started going out with him in January,
we've had around four or five visual encounters
and about three or four non-visual encounters.
and it's been pretty amazing so far.
I'm still hoping for that perfect encounter,
which would be similar to our first encounter,
but only time will tell with that.
If I get another encounter like that, I'm pretty sure we will
because we stay consistently going out as much as possible.
I guess that's why we get to have some of the activity we have
because other people will go out and they're scared and they have fear, rightfully so.
I mean, these creatures are no joke.
But as with anything, I think if you don't provoke them and you're not aggressive, I think they'll be fine.
I think a lot of the things is that they live on fear.
And I think the more you fear them, the more it gets them off, in a sense, or they enjoy it.
where they sense it and love it and try to extort more fear out of you to see how far they can push you.
But they're definitely intelligent.
There's no way around how intelligent they really are.
I think people need to know that.
Yeah, that's a really important element when it comes to these guys that so many people really underestimate.
It good that you mentioned that after having as many encounters with these guys as you had does the anxiety wear off now whenever you have a new experience
Or are you more anxious with new experiences, I should say, as you were when you had your first two encounters?
I would have to say more anxious.
it's definitely there's two aspects to it for me there is the great deal of fear what
the creature could do to me as a human being as far as being like an apex predator
um the size you know the speed the speed is that right there is what i finally got to
see that aspect of it two weekends ago when we had a another visual and non-visual encounter
in the same night that that created a whole different level of fear now
as opposed to the first encounter and some of the other encounters where they've been still
or watching or, you know, just studying you.
But I do get anxious.
I get even more obsessed with it with every encounter I have.
Like, you know, Rick would say, you know, be careful what you wish for.
And I've heard that from a lot of people.
And I hope that one day I don't have an experience where, you know,
I happen to be injured or killed by one of these things or anything like that.
But, you know, before we go out, we typically, you know, pray amongst ourselves for protection and things like that.
I try to always wear my cross necklace and I ask God for his protection.
And, you know, going back to like Martin Groves, you know, his encounter in the LBL where he said that they were coming up on him before he could even move, like to jump in his friend's truck.
He was talking about how he just started praying the Lord's Prayer right there and saying Jesus's name.
And it kind of stopped them, at least in his mind.
And it's what he said that felt like he was given enough time to escape.
So I do think that there's a little bit of a spiritual aspect to these things,
but I do believe that they're more flesh and blood than what we think.
But there is a great deal of fear when I go out there, but a great deal of respect.
because I know with what I'm dealing with, you know, is a very powerful and possibly unhinged creature.
And you don't know what provokes them to violence.
And so you really have to be careful with your way of thinking
because I think that they can sense a lot of different things when you're out there.
It's not just, you know, your physical being,
But I think that they have a greater sense of what's going on in your mind and your emotions.
So once they sense a certain aspect, they, in my opinion, try to use that against you and maybe try to provoke you because they know there's nothing you can really do to hurt them.
That's just how intelligent I think they are.
even from some of the encounters I've heard on your show to other shows,
people who have shot at them and hit them,
and they've basically just shook the shots off or something like that.
It just makes you think that there's really not much you could have on you
to protect yourself against them,
that if they truly want to hurt you, they're going to do it.
but I'm one of those typical people that go out with guns.
I like having the protection.
It's not just for them, but there's other animals out there that can hurt you.
There's mountain lions, wild boars, bears, things like that.
Not so much as bears here in Texas,
but we have mountain lions that are coming into the area now more than ever.
People are seeing them on the hiking trails, and they're sneaky.
I've ran into a pack of wild hogs out at one hiking park.
That, you know, it was a pretty intense experience.
But, you know, I like to have something of safety.
You know, I go out, I carry two firearms.
I carry a small hatchet and a machete and a boot knife and a regular knife in my pocket.
So I'm always armed to the teeth when I go out.
So if I lose it with one thing then I got another thing as a backup So I rather be safe and prepared than not prepared and unsafe
You know what I mean?
I do know what you mean.
And you are well harmed out there.
You don't play around.
You're ready to let the wind out of something.
I don't.
I carry at least, you know, five magazines for my sidearm.
And then I have a Kel-Tec KSG shotgun, 12-gauge, and it's with three-inch shells.
I can, I think, put about 14 to 15 rounds in it, and I carry some extra rounds on me from that.
So I just make sure that I'm well-armed and safe as possible.
And I carry a medic kit, too, so in case one of us gets injured,
I can treat myself or I can treat whoever's with me since 13 years in EMS.
So I like to be safe on that level too.
I always carry a linsatic compass because you can't always rely on other ways of navigation.
So I learned a lot of land navigation day and night when I was in the military.
That was a must.
So I learned to navigate with compass and using my terrain.
So that way when I'm out there, you know, I don't have to rely on so much technology other than like taking pictures or video.
Holy cow, Sean.
When you go out, you are really prepared.
You're ready for anything.
If you've had a dogman encounter and would like to speak with me about it, whether in private or on the show, please go to dogmanencounters.com and submit a report.
All right, Sean, please tell us about your encounters now.
give us every last detail that comes to mind. Well, the first one was around January 5th,
3rd or 5th, somewhere in that area. I can't never remember the exact date, but it was right after
New Year's because right before New Year's, I went out hiking on a small trail north of Six Flags,
about a couple miles north of Six Flags, Fiesta, Texas. And I was actually surprised at what I
found that day on that trail because it happened across a set of prints and they were these huge
five-toe prints and the first one I found on the left side of the trail and from the left side of
the trail to the right side of the trail is about six feet and I found another print so
it had a stride length of almost six feet and it was the first time I ever found any prints
And I got so super excited, took pictures.
And even though the pictures didn't really do the Prince of Justice as seeing them in person, but that trail kind of it's a mile and a half long trail.
It loops around like a U shape.
so when I started following it around after looking through the area a little bit I followed
around and about around the same about the same kind of crossing line I would say from the other
side of the trail on the loop I found another spot where it looked like there was something
that was struggling it was like a couple animals you could see where the grass and stuff was
pressed down and then I saw hog prints and I saw those big footprints again.
And they, uh, I didn't have anything to measure them with other than a water bottle, but they
were about double the length of a 16.9 ounce, uh, water bottle.
Um, so they were huge and they were about half the water, water bottle size and width.
I'd say probably close to six, seven inches in width.
I took some pictures of that
I put my eyewear size 11 boots
so I put my size 11 up against them
and it was almost triple the size of my foot
it was just insane
but it looked like maybe a hog had been
had a little bit of a scuffle
with whatever that was
which I think was a Bigfoot
and I didn't find any blood or anything like that.
I looked, but it looked like, you know, there was a struggle.
Something was drug around, flattened out a lot of the grass,
and then you could see where it was drug up through the tall grass
further up into the wood line and gone.
So I got super excited about that, and I had already met Nick online at that point.
And I told him about that and he was like, well, let's go out this weekend.
He's like, you know, it's going to be decently cold.
He said there is like, you know, they're more active, more active in the cold.
It seems like around here and most of the sightings and reports he had gotten were, you know, throughout the spring and wintertime.
So I was like, yeah, sure.
Let me, you know, get some stuff together.
I wasn't fully prepared for what was about to happen that night.
I didn't have a thermal yet.
I didn't have a really good flashlight.
I just had my firearms from ammo.
I took the best flashlight that I had on me.
And, you know, and that's basically all I had, really.
I hadn't spent a lot of money into any equipment just yet.
And, you know, I didn't know what to expect first time out and how, you know, Nick would play things out, how he wanted to do things.
We went and searched a couple different areas.
there was a picnic area that he was telling me about that the county blocked off for some
unknown reason.
But he had heard that there was an encounter there where a couple was in their car making
out late at night or something.
And one of these dog men came up and shook their car and was messing with their car.
And they had the encounter and took off out of there.
And then not too long after that, the county shut that picnic area down.
And it's still blocked off to this day.
It's been blocked off for, from what I understand, about three years.
And there's no reason for it to be.
There's nothing wrong with the area.
We've gotten up in there many times and there's nothing wrong with it.
We can't find anything wrong with the area.
And we don't understand why they blocked it off.
And it's next to a ranch.
So there's a lot of animal activity too.
But we went and checked that area out that night.
We went and looked at this other creek bed off of 173 going towards Hondo, where Nick works as a handyman.
He has a handyman business, and he deals with a lot of people.
And he gets to talking with them, and they get comfortable with him, and they start telling him things.
So this lady told him a story about she could have swore she saw a werewolf in the middle of the road on 173 one night driving the Honda at 2 o'clock in the morning.
she had drove up and saw this huge hairy dog looking thing in the middle of the road eating a deer
and when she slowed down she said it stood up on two legs and was almost like 10 feet tall
and it just like smiled at her with blood dripping down off its mouth and teeth and everything and it
had these really like bright glowing eyes.
And she said she got so scared that she immediately stopped, turned her car around and went back home.
And so we went down where that sighting was located.
And there's a big creek bed there that when you look at it, it's almost like a super highway that goes up to Lake Medina
and then throughout Hondo, Castroville and all that.
it literally, you know, is a nice little highway to go south.
So we checked that creek bed.
We did find some weird prints.
I found some Bigfoot prints there that was about 16 and a half inches long.
I found another weird print.
I still got pictures of it.
I still can't figure out what kind of print it is.
It's kind of squared off, really small.
The toes are really squared.
it's the weirdest print i've ever seen and it's not very deep into the sand either
still to this day i go back i look at those pictures and i can't figure out what it is
um we found a few dogman tracks that were about the same size as my foot maybe just about a half
inch smaller that could have been like juvenile tracks um we had some rain two nights prior to
So one of the tracks is kind of washed out, but you could still see like three pointed toes up towards the top.
They were pretty close together.
And I'm still it's still a little inconclusive to me, but I save the pictures anyway, because it's best to save as much evidence as you can and go back and look at it.
Ideally, I would love to get a LIDAR scanner, a nice top end LIDAR scanner to take out so we can 3D scan prints and stuff.
and get a better idea other than what my iPhone camera can do.
But so we found all that, and Nick was feeling pretty good that, you know,
maybe we'll have encounters.
I lived in a little bit of fear that night because I wasn't for sure of what I would see
or you know how my mind would treat it if we did have an encounter But he was like well I going to take you out to the Holy Grail area where a lot of people seem to see a lot of things
and that's the Lake Medina spillway a little south of the dam.
There's a little bridge that goes over the spillway there.
And it is by far the most creepiest area ever.
to be in. There's something about it. It's got an ominous feeling when you're there.
It's almost like a perfect choke point, though, when you look at it on a map,
because it backs up to the dam. But it's basically like a, you know, it's cliffed,
got pretty high cliffs on both sides. The curious part of it that I don't really understand
is why whoever the county that runs the dam, I guess,
they've got that entire spillway fenced off with seven to seven and a half foot chain link fence.
And not just the chain link fence itself, but there's razor wire, Constantine wire,
all across the top of that fence, all the way around that area.
like it's holding something in and keeping people out.
But that whole area is one giant choke point, and it's cliffed on both sides,
and there's no real way of getting in there.
They do have signs posted that there's no trespassing, you'll be prosecuted.
And that's the only dam I've ever come across that's been blocked off like that.
And I used to live in Waco before I moved to San Antonio,
and the Waco Dam, I mean, you could hike all around that and have no issue with access to it.
But this one is the first dam I've ever seen that I've been to that has virtually no access to it
unless you're a county employee.
But there's never really anybody there.
And they have signs saying you can kayak or canoe, and that's the only access that you can have.
But there's very little water in that spillway, so you can't really kayak or canoe.
I mean, the deepest that water gets is maybe a foot and a half.
After a good rain, maybe two and a half foot, but nothing major.
So we went out Old Medina Dam Road, and we were looking, you know, for as much as we could, what we could see around the truck.
And as we were coming down the hill from the west side of the dam, I had Nick make sure that he was driving extremely slow so I can look out both windows.
And as we got onto the dam bridge there or the spillway bridge, I happened to be looking out his driver's side window.
and I saw what looked like to be two large glowing green eyes
that were super far apart.
Didn't look like any animal I had ever seen.
And I immediately was like, Nick, Nick, stop, stop, look, look to your left.
And he saw it and he's like, oh my God.
And I was like, we put, he put the truck in park.
I immediately got out.
I walked over to the guardrail of the bridge, and at the distance of me, the bridge is about 15 foot tall.
So at that distance, this thing was literally about 60 feet from us.
But from that height, and it looked super close, but it was so dark down in there, all I could get was kind of an outline of the body and the head.
but the eyes,
the eyes is what captivated me the most.
I could not take my eyes off its eyes.
it was drawing me in emotionally and the way it looked at me.
And when it's,
when you're close up like that,
and I know other people say they,
they're like an Amber color,
but this one,
the color was different.
They did change.
Color, depending on the distance.
Close up, what I saw reminded me of Slimer from Ghostbusters.
That greenish glow.
I'll never forget it.
It's like it's etched in my brain forever.
And it's just how they look at you.
It's hard to describe the feeling you get.
There's a feeling of fear.
There's a feeling of almost sadness.
but intent like it studying you your every movement The way it moved it started to move
at a diagonal angle
backing away from me, but it was so smooth.
It had no
up or down movement. It was just smooth.
It moved ever so slow.
And you could tell it was crouched down quite a bit.
And the further he moved back, the more the eyes glowed more of a yellowish tint.
It was almost, and even the further back he went in the darkness, the eyes were like two big spotlights.
And you could see the shine off the eyes actually shining and refracting off the water.
And I know that no animal can do that.
So Nick, he got his phone out and took some video of it.
And we got about a four-minute video of the eye shine the best we could.
and how it watched us because it moved over to the tree line and brush line in there
and then moved towards our right.
And it was probably, I'd say, about 75 yards away at that point.
And it sat there and it would move back and forth, side to side, watching us
and never took its eyes off of us.
There was a couple times where it blinked enough to where you couldn't see the eye shine or either pass by a tree and maybe block the eye shine.
But it kept its eyes on us the whole time.
I had every hair on my neck standing up.
I was shaking.
I didn't know what to think.
I happened to look to the right of the bridge down the road a little bit and I could have swore I saw
another set of eyes in the tree line there and I told Nick I said I think there's another one
watching us over here and Nick was like well I just saw a set of eyes over here on the left side
of the tree line of the spillway and I was like great so there's three of them and we have one
in the middle seemingly capturing all of our attention while it seems like there's two others
flanking both our sides watching us and not making their presence known so i was like well great
they're strategic i said so you got one trying to kind of lure us out in a sense and then you got
two that's watching us ready to pounce. And Nick's like, yeah. At that point, I got really scared. I
pulled my glock out of my holster, and I started to point it at the one that's in front of us.
And it's like it knew what I had in my hand because it started backing off even further and further.
and once I put it back in the holster, I had almost, I'll be honest with you,
I had almost every intent on taking the shot right there.
But then something in my mind told me not to do it.
So I put the gun back in my holster and it started moving more forward towards us,
a little bit more watching us.
And this encounter lasted for 45 minutes, close to an hour.
It was like we were just having this standoff, watching each other.
It was watching us, studying our movements, and we were doing the same with it.
And there's literally virtually never any traffic in that area,
but for some odd reason, right around that time after I pulled the gun,
within those few minutes after, we could hear traffic.
And the bridge is pretty thin, so it's hard to have two-way traffic going through there, and it's dark.
so if you don't have any lights on you could get hit really quick because people do travel
through there really fast they're not supposed to but they do fly down through there so we hurried
up got in nick's truck and decided to go get out of there um and go check out another area where
he had a client of his who had multiple encounters on his backyard at his deer feeder they were
sitting around his deer feeder at night watching for deer to come in and then attacking the deer
and taken off.
And so we decided to go check that area.
I really wanted to go back to the spillway, but we ended up not going back.
It was about 4 o'clock in the morning at that point, and we were both kind of tired
and exhilarated from our experience, and we just called it a night.

But we kept going back and Nick had bought a drone so we got back there one other night I think the following month in February we caught some eyeshine that was about 30 foot up in a tree and it was watching us
And that lasted for another 45 minutes to an hour where whatever that was, was watching us.
It was at enough of a distance, but the eyeshine was so bright and so big.
we were like it's got to be one that's climbed up in a tree and is watching us and nick flew the
drone over there and what's weird is the video capability of the drone malfunctioned for some
odd reason once he got close to it so he couldn't take video but he was able to get still shots of
the eyeshine and then the drone just started acting funny so he he brought it home and
caught it a night with that. We got that eyeshine that night.
That was a very weird night too because we ended up getting out of there
again and
went and checked out another area.

we went to a different spot
in the area that has a small river and a bridge
and we're starting to kind of notice a little bit of a similar pattern of some of these areas.

but always close to water and very vegetative.
The foliage was super thick and very dark.
we found
some more prints
Nick found
there's a cliffside
next to this bridge
it's probably about 100 foot tall
and there's a
house that sits up on top of the cliff
that's always
lit up but even
though that's lit up you can't see anything
on the cliffside and there's so much foliage
that covers the cliffside we didn't realize
there was an outcropping of the
bottom part of the cliff there that gives good protection from rain and weather.
Nick ended up going in there.

And it's what's weird about it is you can't see into it because of the foliage during the day or night.
But you can see out of it very well.
it's it's just crazy so it it seemed like it was like like you know a sentry bed or something like
they'd have like one of the pack lay there and watch the opening to the area as a guard and then
if there was more they slept further back in there when we got in there we were finding weird

got to be a den site that they sleep in or something or stay in at times but
there was animal bones everywhere we took pictures of all that got video

The weird claw marks in the ground, that's what's weird to me because I've heard of how their front hands are kind of like similar to raccoon paws in a sense, but look more like human hands with long claws.
Anyway, you can see these clear, thick claw marks in the sand in there.
like they're walking on all fours because we can't really if they're that as tall as they are
uh staying on bipedally the the height in there is not going to do them any good walking bipedal so
um we're pretty sure that at least in our minds that those markings were possibly their front
paws or their front hands with the claws digging into the sand as they would maneuver through there.
We went in there with his dog, Darcy, and she was acting weird.
The whole time we were in there on one of the other occasions, because we go there to that den site every time we're out just to check it out and see if there's anything new.
We'll move things around too in there to see if they move items.
I did put a trail camera in there for a week and didn't have much luck with it.
